Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium, Dharamsala
Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow

storylines.title: PBKS lose 4 in a row — Madhav Tiwari, Auqib Nabi cameos seal Dharamsala heist
storylines.body: Punjab Kings have completed an unthinkable collapse. From 6-0-1 on April 19, they've lost 4 in a row to slip to 4th. At Dharamsala on May 11, Priyansh Arya's 24-ball fifty had PBKS at 210/5 — should have been winning. DC chased it with 6 balls to spare, thanks to a series of unlikely heroes: Ashutosh Sharma 24 off 10 (smashed Yash Thakur for two sixes in the death), Madhav Tiwari 18* (Player of the Match), and Auqib Nabi 10* who hit a free-hit four after a no-ball to all but seal it. Iyer admitted post-match: 'Don't have to beat around the bush — fielding and bowling again. Feel it was 30 runs more on this wicket.' PBKS dropped two catches and misfielded repeatedly. The bowling — the season's structural flaw — has now leaked 222, 235 (in vain), 216 (chased) across the slide. NRR has crashed from +1.333 to +0.428. With 3 games left (MI, GT, SRH), they need to win at least 2 to confirm top 4. The 'chokers' label is back in the conversation.

storylines.title: Bhuvneshwar Kumar 4/23 + last-ball six — RCB heist MI off the final delivery at Raipur
body: Royal Challengers Bengaluru pulled off a 2-wicket win off the LAST BALL at Raipur on May 10 in their first home game at the venue in a decade. Bhuvneshwar Kumar took 4/23 (Player of the Match, plus Cricinfo's MVP at 144.46 pts) AND hit the winning runs — only the 4th six of his entire IPL career, his first since 2016, an inside-out drive over cover off Raj Bawa to clinch it. The match got off to a brutal start for RCB though: Virat Kohli was dismissed for a GOLDEN DUCK by Deepak Chahar — a mistimed lofted drive caught at mid-off by Raj Bawa — making it back-to-back ducks for Kohli after his 2-ball nought to Prince Yadav vs LSG. Krunal Pandya played the innings of his life on the way: 73 off 46 fighting cramps so bad he was on the floor between balls. MI's Bumrah bowled a sensational 19th but Raj Bawa misfielded the final ball, gifting the winning run. An earlier overstep no-ball by Bawa also gifted RCB a free hit at a critical moment. RCB go top of the table at 14 points, NRR +1.103. MI ELIMINATED.

storylines.title: Kohli back-to-back ducks — 2-ball duck vs LSG, golden duck vs MI in consecutive games
storylines.body: Virat Kohli's miserable week continued at Raipur. Two days after his first 2-ball duck while chasing in 9 years (bowled by Prince Yadav at Ekana), Kohli walked back for a GOLDEN DUCK the very next match — caught at mid-off by Raj Bawa off Deepak Chahar in the third ball of RCB's innings. He played a mistimed lofted drive, the bat face closing and the ball hitting the inside half, ballooning to catchable height. Back-to-back ducks for IPL's GOAT — a stat that would have been unimaginable a month ago. Even more painful for RCB fans: Kohli still leads the Orange Cap top 5 with 379 runs but his SR has dipped sharply, and dismissals are coming early. The 9-year streak of NO 2-ball chasing ducks ended Thursday; the back-to-back ducks add to a rough patch right when RCB need their talisman at the top to fire for the playoffs.

storylines.title: Urvil Patel's 13-ball fifty equals IPL record — CSK's third-highest chase ever knocks out LSG
storylines.body: Urvil Patel walked into the middle at Chepauk on May 10 with CSK's chances of winning at 38%. He walked out 23 balls and 65 runs later with the chances at 93%. His 13-ball fifty equalled Yashasvi Jaiswal's all-time IPL record — eight sixes off LSG's spin and pace, five sixes off five consecutive balls at one point, the crowd chanting his name. Earlier, Josh Inglis had pulverised CSK's bowling with 85 off 33, but Jamie Overton (3/36, Player of the Match) and Anshul Kamboj (2/47) kept LSG to 203/8. Shivam Dube finished with two sixes off the last over. CSK's 208/5 chased target of 204 — their third-highest successful IPL chase ever, behind two 206 chases against RCB. LSG officially ELIMINATED. CSK on a 3-match winning streak, sit 5th with 12 points, alive in the playoff race. Dhoni still not in the XI.

storylines.title: Rashid Khan 4/33 wrecks RR — GT's fourth straight win lifts them to top of the table
storylines.body: Gujarat Titans dismantled Rajasthan Royals by 77 runs at Jaipur on May 9 to climb to second place (now joint-top after results) with 14 points. Shubman Gill (84) and Sai Sudharsan (55) put on a 118-run opening stand to power GT to 229/4 — their biggest IPL score outside Ahmedabad. RR's chase looked promising at 86/3 in 7 overs before Rashid Khan came on and turned the game on its head: 4/33 (Player of the Match), the first scalp of Dhruv Jurel on 24, then ripping through the middle order. Yashasvi Jaiswal was captaining RR for the first time after Riyan Parag's hamstring injury — Parag had scored 90 in the previous match before going down. Jadeja's 38 was the only resistance as RR were bowled out for 152. GT's 4th straight win has them firmly in the top 4 conversation.

storylines.title: MI and LSG officially eliminated — two billionaire-team disappointments of IPL 2026
storylines.body: It's official: Mumbai Indians and Lucknow Super Giants are out of IPL 2026 playoffs. MI's last-ball loss to RCB at Raipur on May 10 (despite Krunal Pandya's heroic 73 with cramps) sealed their fate at 3-8 from 11 games. LSG's 5-wicket loss to CSK at Chepauk the same day, after starting 2-0 promisingly, ended their slide at 3-8. The biggest disappointments of the season: LSG's Rs.27Cr captain Rishabh Pant has averaged poorly with the bat. Nicholas Pooran is among the worst-performing high-priced overseas buys at 70+ runs in 11 innings; only Mitchell Marsh's 111 vs RCB kept LSG's season memorable. MI's Bumrah-Boult-Hardik core, despite Bumrah's brilliance, couldn't lift them past mid-table. Both teams now play out the string with one eye on the 2027 auction.
